one_line_thesis: Global industrial gases oligopoly with stable on-site/merchant core; watch at ~$297 until clean-energy write-downs end and owner cash growth resumes (base ~1% annual return).

deep_dive.executive_summary: Air Products is a global industrial gases oligopoly with long-lived on-site contracts and merchant pricing power, now resetting capital allocation after expensive clean-energy project write-downs. At $296.75 (NYSE, 2026-07-10), base expected annual return at today's price is 1% per year on normalized maintenance-level owner cash ($8.62 per share), far below our 15% bar. Durable competitive advantage (Munger moat) is stable in core gases but tested by repeated project impairments. Asymmetric payoff (Pabrai dhando) is partial: the $7.14 annual dividend (43-year increase streak) and investment...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Predictable cash-flow security
applicability_tests: Incremental capital can be tied to identifiable projects, cohorts, or contracted capacity.
Returns on new capital can be separated from returns on the installed base.
Contract duration, volume protection, pricing, and counterparty risk are observable.
Leverage and future owner-funded capital are explicitly deducted.
failure_modes: contract renewal or regulatory reset ignored

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

timeline.label: Net income moved -89.7% versus the prior period (3,828.2 to 394.5).
Board/CEO review exits Louisiana Clean Energy Complex, Casa Grande green hydrogen, and smaller clean-energy projects; expects up to $2.9B pre-tax charge in fiscal Q3 2026
Q2 FY2026: sales $3.2B (+9% YoY); adjusted EPS $3.20 (+19% vs prior-year quarter); operating recovery after FY2025 charges
FY2025: $3.7B pre-tax business and asset actions (World Energy SAF exit, project exits); GAAP loss $1.74/share; adjusted EPS $12.03
Accelerated clean hydrogen and NEOM green ammonia investments while maintaining core on-site/merchant gases
Sold LNG process technology and equipment business; recognized ~$1.6B pre-tax gain in Q4 FY2024
Follow-on $908M investment in JIGPC joint venture (55% interest) for Middle East industrial gases
Initial $1.6B investment in JIGPC joint venture
NEOM Green Hydrogen Company joint venture formed for Saudi green ammonia facility (VIE consolidation)
Seifi Ghasemi became Chairman, President and CEO; shifted strategy toward energy and environmental markets
Board authorized ongoing share repurchase program (balance used opportunistically)
43 consecutive annual dividend increases through FY2025 ($7.14/share annual)
Company founded; built global industrial gases franchise (on-site, merchant, packaged)

industry.structure: Global industrial gases is an oligopoly (Air Products, Linde, Air Liquide) with high capital barriers, long-lived on-site plants, and contractual pass-through of energy costs. Switching costs are high for large customers.
industry.share_shift: Air Products is reallocating capital away from U.S. clean-energy megaprojects (Louisiana, Casa Grande) back toward core gases; peers compete on pricing discipline and project returns in hydrogen.
industry.trend: Energy transition drives hydrogen and carbon-capture project pipelines, but returns scrutiny has intensified after large impairments; helium merchant remains volatile while non-helium pricing held in FY2025.
